meaning: n.爆炸,勃然大怒,叱责,[照相]放大,崩溃
examples: Izola's main dining room sets large blowup photos of former Chicago Mayor Harold Washington and other local luminaries.
He and Cohen appeared to be heading for a major blowup.
Frank and I had a blowup last night.
In the inflationary blowup of the late 1970s, diamonds were the subject of considerable investment fervor.
Mall REITs' stats have suffered from the blowup of one prominent member, Mills Corp.
